facebook.content_angle: Six-beat kick technique — most club swimmers default to a two-beat kick and don't realise they're leaving a measurable speed gain on the table
facebook.post_text:
Your kick is costing you a full second per lap. 🦵

Most club swimmers default to a two-beat kick without knowing it — one kick per arm pull, efficient but nowhere near fast enough for race pace.

👉 Intermediate swimmers cut their **50m time trials by 10.21% in just four weeks** of six-beat kick training — that's a full second in a 100m
👉 A six-beat pattern (three kicks per arm cycle) generates more propulsion and stops your hips sinking mid-stroke
👉 Try this: kick only for 6×50m counting

at race tempo — then carry that rhythm into full-stroke

Most swimmers have been on cruise control with their kick for years and never knew. 😳

✔️ Practice the six-beat in low-intensity sets first — it takes 3–4 weeks to feel natural
✔️ Kick from your **hips, not your knees** — bent knees kill the power transfer
✔️ For distance events use two-beat for efficiency, switch to six-beat in the final 25m sprint

Have you ever deliberately trained your kick tempo — or has it just been whatever feels natural? 👇
facebook.engagement_bait: Drop a 🦵 if your kick has always just been 'whatever feels natural' — you're not alone.
facebook.call_to_action: Have you ever deliberately trained your kick tempo — or has it just been whatever feels natural?
